---
title: "CCA on people with SD in CBs"
author: Arturo Bertero
output: 
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    code_folding: show
date: "2024-12-11"
---

# Input

```{r}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  results = "markup", # Show output
  warning = FALSE,  # Do not show warnings
  message = FALSE   # Do not show messages
)

```


```{r}
#Libraries
library(pacman)
p_load(tidyverse, janitor, here, haven, labelled, sjmisc, skimr, ggplot2, jtools,
       stargazer, qgraph, Matrix, igraph, sna, NetworkComparisonTest, bootnet,
       rio, foreign, matrixcalc, corclass, NetworkToolbox, conflicted, 
       EGAnet, texreg, ggpubr, lavaan, ltm, kableExtra, sjPlot, psych, nFactors,
       ggcorrplot, ggeffects, summarytools, GGally, factoextra, cluster)

#Options
rm(list = ls())
options(scipen = 999)

conflict_prefer("select", "dplyr")
conflict_prefer("filter", "dplyr")
conflict_prefer("lag", "dplyr")
conflict_prefer("labelled", "to_factor")
conflict_prefer("igraph", "compose")
conflict_prefer("igraph", "%s%")
```

```{r}
# Import cleaned data
data = readRDS(here("Input", "Secondary", "data.rds")) 
country_data = readRDS(here("Input", "Secondary", "country_data.rds"))
```

```{r}
# Country_data without GCB
country_data = lapply(country_data, function(df) {
  df %>%
    select(-GCB)  
})

#Country_data obj as matrix
country_data <- lapply(country_data, function(sublist) as.matrix(sublist))
```

```{r}
# data with nested country lists
# Initialize the new list
country_data_full <- list()

# Split the data based on country
country_data_full <- split(data, data$country)
country_data_full <- lapply(country_data_full, as_tibble)
```

```{r}
# objects for network 
(shortnames = c("rad_poison","aids_cia","elf_mindcontrol","chemtrails",
              "un_worldgov","rothschild_control","monsanto_gmo","ebola_bigpharma"))

# Country color palette 
country_colors <- c(
  "DEU" = "#E41A1C", "ESP" = "#377EB8", "FRA" = "#4DAF4A", 
  "GBR" = "#984EA3", "ITA" = "#FF7F00", "POL" = "#ffcf33", "SWE" = "#A65628"
)

#Totalgroup_comm <- list(
#  "Conspiracy Beliefs" = c(1:8),
#  "Conspiracy Mentality" = c(9))
#
#Totalgroup_cols <- c("#966FD6B3", "#008B8BB3") 

```

# Processing

```{r}
# Lists for ppl with SD versus no SD

# Initialize new list
country_data_full_nosd <- list()  # Stores rows with no SD across cb_1 to cb_8

# Loop through each country in country_data_full
for (country in names(country_data_full)) {
  
  # Access the current country's tibble
  country_tibble <- country_data_full[[country]]
  
  # Filter rows where all values in cb_1 to cb_8 are the same
  country_data_full_nosd[[country]] <- country_tibble %>%
    filter(apply(select(., cb_1:cb_8), 1, function(row) length(unique(row)) == 1))  # Rows with uniform values
  
  # Retain rows where not all values in cb_1 to cb_8 are the same
  country_data_full[[country]] <- country_tibble %>%
    filter(apply(select(., cb_1:cb_8), 1, function(row) length(unique(row)) > 1))  # Rows with non-uniform values
}

```

```{r}
# Remove no SD from country data

# Initialize the new list
country_data <- list()

# Loop through each unique country in country_data_full
for (country in names(country_data_full)) {
  
  # Access the tibble for the current country
  country_tibble <- country_data_full[[country]]
  
  # Select only cb_1 to cb_8 columns
  country_cb <- country_tibble %>%
    select(cb_1:cb_8)
  
  # Transform into a matrix
  country_data[[country]] <- as.matrix(country_cb)
}
```

## Full sample EGA for people with SD

```{r}
# Initialize a list to store the EGA results and plots
EGA_full <- list()
EGA_plots <- list()

# Loop through each element in country_data
for (name in names(country_data)) {
  
  # Access the data matrix
  data_matrix <- country_data[[name]]
  
  # Fit the EGA model using glasso (partial correlation network)
  ega_result <- EGA(
    data_matrix,
    model = "glasso",
    uni.method = "louvain",
    plot.EGA = F
  )
  
  # Extract the plot object
  ega_plot <- plot(ega_result)
  
  # Add the title to the plot using ggplot2's ggtitle
  ega_plot <- ega_plot + ggplot2::ggtitle(paste("EGA full sample -", name))
  
  # Print the modified plot
  print(ega_plot)
  
  # Store the EGA result in a list for later use
  EGA_full[[name]] <- ega_result
}

```


## CCA for people with SD

```{r message=TRUE}
# CCA by country

# list 
cca = list()

# Loop 
for (country in names(country_data)) {
  
  # Fit the CCA model for the current country
  cca_result <- cca(
    country_data[[country]], 
    filter.significance = TRUE, 
    filter.value = 0.05, 
    zero.action = c("ownclass")
  )
  
  # Store the CCA results in the list
  cca[[country]] <- cca_result
}

```

```{r}
# Add membership as a col in country_data_full

for (country in names(country_data_full)) {
  # Retrieve membership vector
  membership_vector <- cca[[country]][["membership"]]
  
  # Add 
  country_data_full[[country]] <- country_data_full[[country]] %>%
    mutate(membership = membership_vector)
}

```

```{r}
# split country_data_full based on CCA results

# list 
country_data_split <- list()

# Loop through country_data_full
for (country in names(country_data_full)) {
  
  # selected columns 
  country_data <- country_data_full[[country]] %>%
    select(cb_1:cb_8, membership)
  
  # Split the rows 
  split_data <- country_data %>%
    group_by(membership) %>%
    group_split()
  
  # Convert into matrix, remove membership
  for (group in split_data) {
    membership_value <- unique(group$membership)
    matrix_name <- paste0(country, "_", membership_value)
    
    # Convert to matrix 
    country_data_split[[matrix_name]] <- as.matrix(group %>% select(-membership))
  }
}
```

### Correlation matrices for each CCA cluster

```{r}
# Initialize lists to store correlation matrices and plots
cor_matrices <- list()
correlation_plots <- list()

# Loop through each element in country_data_split
for (name in names(country_data_split)) {
  
  # Access the data matrix
  data_matrix <- country_data_split[[name]]
  
  # Compute correlation matrix
  corr_matrix <- round(cor(data_matrix), 2)
  
  # Store the correlation matrix in cor_matrices
  cor_matrices[[name]] <- corr_matrix
  
  # Create the correlation plot
  correlation_plots[[name]] <- ggcorrplot(
    corr_matrix, 
    type = "lower", 
    legend.title = " ", 
    outline.col = "white", 
    colors = c("red", "white", "blue"),
    lab = TRUE   # Show correlation values
  ) +
    ggtitle(name) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 17, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      plot.margin = margin(2, 2, 2, 2, "mm"),
      axis.text.x = element_text(margin = margin(-2, 0, 0, 0)),  # Order: top, right, bottom, left
      axis.text.y = element_text(margin = margin(0, -2, 0, 0))
    ) +
    scale_x_discrete(labels = shortnames[-1]) +  # Exclude the first label for diagonal
    scale_y_discrete(labels = shortnames[-length(shortnames)]) +  # Exclude the last label for diagonal
    geom_vline(xintercept = 1:ncol(corr_matrix) - 0.5, colour = "white", size = 2) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 1:ncol(corr_matrix) - 0.5, colour = "white", size = 2)
}

# Print all correlation plots
for (name in names(correlation_plots)) {
  print(correlation_plots[[name]])
}


```


### Are CCA clusters suitable for factor analysis? 

```{r}
# Initialize an empty data frame to store results
suitable_df <- data.frame(
  Case = character(),
  Kaiser = character(),
  Bartlett = character(),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Loop through each element in country_data_split
for (name in names(country_data_split)) {
  
  # Access the data matrix
  data_matrix <- country_data_split[[name]]
  
  # Kaiser Test
  kmo_result <- tryCatch({
    kmo <- KMO(cor(data_matrix))
    if (kmo$MSA >= 0.6) "Passed" else "Not passed"
  }, error = function(e) "Not passed")
  
  # Bartlett's Test
  bartlett_result <- tryCatch({
    bartlett <- cortest.bartlett(cor(data_matrix), n = nrow(data_matrix))
    if (bartlett$p.value < 0.05) "Passed" else "Not passed"
  }, error = function(e) "Not passed")
  
  # Add results to the data frame
  suitable_df <- rbind(
    suitable_df,
    data.frame(Case = name, Kaiser = kmo_result, Bartlett = bartlett_result, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  )
}
```


```{r}
# Print
suitable_df %>%
  kable("html", caption = "Kaiser and Bartlett tests") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = "striped",
                full_width = FALSE, 
                position = "center")
```

### EGA of each CCA cluster

```{r}
# Initialize lists to store the EGA results and plots
EGA <- list()

# Loop through each element in country_data_split
for (name in names(country_data_split)) {
  
  # Access the data matrix
  data_matrix <- country_data_split[[name]]
  
  # Fit the EGA model using glasso (partial correlation network)
  ega_result <- EGA(
    data_matrix,
    model = "glasso",
    uni.method = "louvain",
    plot.EGA = F  # Disable automatic plotting
  )
  
  # Extract the plot object
  ega_plot <- plot(ega_result)
  
  # Add the title to the plot using ggplot2's ggtitle
  ega_plot <- ega_plot + ggplot2::ggtitle(paste("EGA -", name))
  
  # Print the modified plot
  print(ega_plot)
  
  # Store the EGA result in a list for later use
  EGA[[name]] <- ega_result
  
}

```

### Cluster analysis of CCA correlation matrieces 

```{r}
# Step 1: Extract and flatten upper triangle of correlation matrices
adjacency_vectors <- lapply(names(cor_matrices), function(country) {
  matrix <- cor_matrices[[country]]  # Extract the correlation matrix
  upper_triangle <- matrix[upper.tri(matrix)]  # Extract the upper triangle without the diagonal
  c(upper_triangle)  # Flatten into a vector
})

# Step 2: Combine into a data frame
adjacency_df <- do.call(rbind, adjacency_vectors) %>% as.data.frame()
rownames(adjacency_df) <- names(cor_matrices)

# Step 3: Determine the optimal number of clusters using gap statistic
set.seed(123)  # Ensure reproducibility
gap_stat <- clusGap(
  adjacency_df, 
  FUN = kmeans, 
  K.max = 10,  # Maximum number of clusters to evaluate
  B = 500      # Number of bootstrap iterations
)

# Visualize the gap statistic
fviz_gap_stat(gap_stat)

# Step 4: Find the optimal number of clusters using the one-standard-error rule
optimal_clusters <- maxSE(gap_stat$Tab[, "gap"], gap_stat$Tab[, "SE.sim"], method = "firstSEmax")

# Print the optimal number of clusters
cat("Optimal number of clusters:", optimal_clusters, "\n")

# Step 5: Perform k-means clustering with the optimal number of clusters
set.seed(123)
kmeans_result <- kmeans(adjacency_df, centers = optimal_clusters)

# Step 6: Add cluster assignments to the countries
cluster_assignments <- data.frame(
  Country = names(cor_matrices),
  Cluster = kmeans_result$cluster
)

# Print cluster assignments
print(cluster_assignments)

# Step 7: Optional visualization
fviz_cluster(kmeans_result, data = adjacency_df, geom = "point", ellipse.type = "convex")
```

### Cluster analysis of partial correlation (EGA) matrieces

```{r}
# Step 1: Extract and flatten upper triangle of adjacency matrices
adjacency_vectors <- lapply(names(EGA), function(country) {
  matrix <- EGA[[country]][["network"]]  # Extract the adjacency matrix
  upper_triangle <- matrix[upper.tri(matrix)]  # Extract the upper triangle without the diagonal
  c(upper_triangle)  # Flatten into a vector
})

# Step 2: Combine into a data frame
adjacency_df <- do.call(rbind, adjacency_vectors) %>% as.data.frame()
rownames(adjacency_df) <- names(EGA)

# Step 3: Determine the optimal number of clusters using gap statistic
set.seed(123)  # Ensure reproducibility
gap_stat <- clusGap(
  adjacency_df, 
  FUN = kmeans, 
  K.max = 10,  # Maximum number of clusters to evaluate
  B = 500      # Number of bootstrap iterations
)

# Visualize the gap statistic
fviz_gap_stat(gap_stat)

# Step 4: Find the optimal number of clusters using the one-standard-error rule
optimal_clusters <- maxSE(gap_stat$Tab[, "gap"], gap_stat$Tab[, "SE.sim"], method = "firstSEmax")

# Print the optimal number of clusters
cat("Optimal number of clusters:", optimal_clusters, "\n")

# Step 5: Perform k-means clustering with the optimal number of clusters
set.seed(123)
kmeans_result <- kmeans(adjacency_df, centers = optimal_clusters)

# Step 6: Add cluster assignments to the countries
cluster_assignments <- data.frame(
  Country = names(EGA),
  Cluster = kmeans_result$cluster
)

# Print cluster assignments
print(cluster_assignments)

# Step 7: Optional visualization
fviz_cluster(kmeans_result, data = adjacency_df, geom = "point", ellipse.type = "convex")
```

### EFA

```{r}
# Initialize the EFA result list
EFA <- list()

# Loop through each matrix in country_data_split
for (name in names(country_data_split)) {
  
  # Access the data matrix
  data_matrix <- country_data_split[[name]]
  
  # Check if the matrix has enough rows and columns for EFA
  if (ncol(data_matrix) > 1 && nrow(data_matrix) > ncol(data_matrix)) {
    # Store EFA results for 1 and 2 factors
    efa_results <- list(
      Factors_1 = fa(
        data_matrix,
        nfactors = 1,  # 1-factor solution
        rotate = "varimax",
        fm = "ml"
      ),
      Factors_2 = fa(
        data_matrix,
        nfactors = 2,  # 2-factor solution
        rotate = "varimax",
        fm = "ml"
      )
    )
  } else {
    efa_results <- NULL
  }
  
  # Store the results for each country
  EFA[[name]] <- efa_results
}
```

```{r}
# Empty list to store stats
efa_stats_list <- list()

# Loop 
for (name in names(EFA)) {
  
  # Access the 1-factor and 2-factor results
  factors_1 <- EFA[[name]][["Factors_1"]]
  factors_2 <- EFA[[name]][["Factors_2"]]
  
  # Extract stats 
  tli_1 <- factors_1[["TLI"]]
  cfi_1 <- factors_1[["CFI"]]
  rmsea_1 <- factors_1[["RMSEA"]][["RMSEA"]]
  bic_1 <- factors_1[["BIC"]]
  
  # Extract for 2-factor 
  tli_2 <- factors_2[["TLI"]]
  cfi_2 <- factors_2[["CFI"]]
  rmsea_2 <- factors_2[["RMSEA"]][["RMSEA"]]
  bic_2 <- factors_2[["BIC"]]
  
  # Store 
  efa_stats_list[[name]] <- data.frame(
    Country_Membership = name,
    TLI_1 = tli_1,
    CFI_1 = cfi_1,
    RMSEA_1 = rmsea_1,
    BIC_1 = bic_1,
    TLI_2 = tli_2,
    CFI_2 = cfi_2,
    RMSEA_2 = rmsea_2,
    BIC_2 = bic_2
  )
}

# Combine the list into a single data frame
EFA_stats <- do.call(rbind, efa_stats_list)


```

```{r}
# Initialize an empty list to store stats
efa_stats_list <- list()

# Loop through each element in EFA
for (name in names(EFA)) {
  
  # Access the 1-factor and 2-factor results
  factors_1 <- EFA[[name]][["Factors_1"]]
  factors_2 <- EFA[[name]][["Factors_2"]]
  
  # Extract relevant stats for 1-factor solution
  tli_1 <- factors_1[["TLI"]]
  cfi_1 <- factors_1[["CFI"]]
  rmsea_1 <- factors_1[["RMSEA"]][["RMSEA"]]
  bic_1 <- factors_1[["BIC"]]
  
  # Extract relevant stats for 2-factor solution
  tli_2 <- factors_2[["TLI"]]
  cfi_2 <- factors_2[["CFI"]]
  rmsea_2 <- factors_2[["RMSEA"]][["RMSEA"]]
  bic_2 <- factors_2[["BIC"]]
  
  # Determine the winner
  # Criteria: Higher TLI and CFI, lower RMSEA and BIC are better
  score_1 <- (tli_1 > tli_2) + (cfi_1 > cfi_2) + (rmsea_1 < rmsea_2) + (bic_1 < bic_2)
  score_2 <- (tli_2 > tli_1) + (cfi_2 > cfi_1) + (rmsea_2 < rmsea_1) + (bic_2 < bic_1)
  
  # Tie-breaking rule: Prefer 1-factor solution in case of a tie
  winner <- ifelse(score_1 > score_2, 1, ifelse(score_2 > score_1, 2, 1))
  
  # Store the results in a list
  efa_stats_list[[name]] <- data.frame(
    Country_Membership = name,
    TLI_1 = tli_1,
    CFI_1 = cfi_1,
    RMSEA_1 = rmsea_1,
    BIC_1 = bic_1,
    TLI_2 = tli_2,
    CFI_2 = cfi_2,
    RMSEA_2 = rmsea_2,
    BIC_2 = bic_2,
    Winner = winner  # Add the winner column
  )
}

# Combine the list into a single data frame
EFA_stats <- do.call(rbind, efa_stats_list)

# Check the resulting data frame
EFA_stats %>%
  kable("html", caption = "EFA fit statistics") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = "responsive",
                full_width = FALSE, 
                position = "center")

```

### CFA

```{r}
# Initialize the CFA result list
CFA <- list()

# Loop through each element in country_data_split
for (name in names(country_data_split)) {
  
  # Access the data matrix
  data_matrix <- country_data_split[[name]]
  
  # Access the winner (1 or 2 factors) for the corresponding element
  winner <- efa_stats_list[[name]][["Winner"]]
  
  # Specify CFA model based on the winner
  if (winner == 1) {
    # 1-factor model: All variables load on Factor 1
    model <- paste0("Factor1 =~ ", paste(colnames(data_matrix), collapse = " + "))
  } else if (winner == 2) {
    # 2-factor model: Assign variables to Factor 1 or Factor 2 based on loadings
    loadings <- EFA[[name]][["Factors_2"]][["loadings"]]  # Extract the loadings matrix
    
    # Keep only rows corresponding to variable names
    loadings <- loadings[rownames(loadings) %in% colnames(data_matrix), , drop = FALSE]
    
    # Determine which factor each variable loads onto
    factor_1_vars <- rownames(loadings)[apply(loadings, 1, function(row) which.max(row) == 1)]
    factor_2_vars <- rownames(loadings)[apply(loadings, 1, function(row) which.max(row) == 2)]
    
    # Check if any factor is empty
    if (length(factor_1_vars) == 0 || length(factor_2_vars) == 0) {
      warning(paste("Skipping CFA for", name, "due to empty factor assignment."))
      CFA[[name]] <- NULL
      next
    }
    
    # Build the 2-factor model
    model <- paste0(
      "Factor1 =~ ", paste(factor_1_vars, collapse = " + "), "\n",
      "Factor2 =~ ", paste(factor_2_vars, collapse = " + ")
    )
  }
  
  # Fit the CFA model
  cfa_result <- tryCatch(
    cfa(model, data = as.data.frame(data_matrix)),
    error = function(e) {
      warning(paste("CFA failed for", name, ":", e$message))
      return(NULL)
    }
  )
  
  # Store the CFA result
  CFA[[name]] <- cfa_result
}

```

```{r}
# Initialize an empty list to store CFA statistics
CFA_stats_list <- list()

# Loop through each element in the CFA list
for (name in names(CFA)) {
  
  # Check if the CFA result exists (is not NULL)
  if (!is.null(CFA[[name]])) {
    # Check if the model converged
    converged <- inspect(CFA[[name]], "converged")
    
    if (converged) {
      # Extract fit measures
      fit_measures <- fitMeasures(CFA[[name]])
      
      # Extract relevant statistics
      chisq <- fit_measures["chisq"]
      p_value <- fit_measures["pvalue"]
      cfi <- fit_measures["cfi"]
      tli <- fit_measures["tli"]
      rmsea <- fit_measures["rmsea"]
      srmr <- fit_measures["srmr"]
      bic <- fit_measures["bic"]
      
      # Store statistics in a data frame
      CFA_stats_list[[name]] <- data.frame(
        Country_Membership = name,
        Chi_Square = chisq,
        P_Value = p_value,
        CFI = cfi,
        TLI = tli,
        RMSEA = rmsea,
        SRMR = srmr,
        BIC = bic,
        Converged = TRUE,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
    } else {
      # Model did not converge, store NA values
      CFA_stats_list[[name]] <- data.frame(
        Country_Membership = name,
        Chi_Square = NA,
        P_Value = NA,
        CFI = NA,
        TLI = NA,
        RMSEA = NA,
        SRMR = NA,
        BIC = NA,
        Converged = FALSE,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
    }
  } else {
    # If CFA result is NULL, store NA values
    CFA_stats_list[[name]] <- data.frame(
      Country_Membership = name,
      Chi_Square = NA,
      P_Value = NA,
      CFI = NA,
      TLI = NA,
      RMSEA = NA,
      SRMR = NA,
      BIC = NA,
      Converged = FALSE,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  }
}

```

```{r}
# Combine all statistics into a single data frame (optional)
CFA_stats <- do.call(rbind, CFA_stats_list)

# Add a Fit column to the CFA_stats dataframe with three categories
CFA_stats <- CFA_stats %>%
  mutate(
    Fit = case_when(
      # If the model did not converge, mark as "Bad"
      Converged == FALSE ~ "Bad",
      # Classify as "Good" if all stringent criteria are met
      Converged == TRUE & 
        CFI >= 0.95 & TLI >= 0.95 & RMSEA <= 0.05 & SRMR <= 0.05 ~ "Good",
      # Classify as "Mediocre" if acceptable but not good
      Converged == TRUE & 
        CFI >= 0.90 & TLI >= 0.90 & RMSEA <= 0.08 & SRMR <= 0.08 ~ "Mediocre",
      # Otherwise, classify as "Bad"
      TRUE ~ "Bad"
    )
  )

#Print
CFA_stats %>%
  kable("html", caption = "Suitability Results") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = "responsive",
                full_width = FALSE, 
                position = "center")

```
